response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack engineer with web/microservices background, but the role requires 8 years of C++ as a hard minimum and the resume shows only ~6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments back in 2014–2015. C++ is not even listed in the skills section. Core stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang. While there is relevant high-throughput distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, the C++ gap and absence of a formal tech-lead role on a cloud compute platform make this a clear miss for this specific JD.
strengths: High-scale distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/sec, low-latency services)
Long track record of software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad polyglot background (DLang, TypeScript, Java, C#) with infra/devops exposure (Kubernetes, Docker, GitLab CI)
concerns: Falls far short of the 8 years C++ minimum — only a brief C/C++ stint a decade ago and C++ absent from listed skills
No demonstrated production cloud compute platform architecture ownership
No formal technical leadership title or evidence of leading a platform's technical strategy across teams

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform / tech-lead ownership
